name: Telling Time — O'clock and Half Past
whatIsIt: A clock has two hands. The SHORT hand shows the HOUR. The LONG hand shows the MINUTES. When the long hand points straight up to 12, it is exactly 'o'clock' (like 3 o'clock). When the long hand points straight down to 6, half the hour has gone — that is 'half past' (like half past 3, or 3:30). There are 60 minutes in one hour.
whyNeeded: Time runs a child's whole day — school at 8 o'clock, lunch at half past 12, cartoons at 5. Reading a clock lets them follow a routine, be on time, and understand 'before' and 'after' in real life.
realLifeUses: A TV show at half past 5

vocabulary.meaning: The SHORT hand — it points to the hour.
The LONG hand — it points to the minutes.
When the long hand points to 12 — a whole hour, no extra minutes.
When the long hand points to 6 — 30 minutes past the hour.

story.title: Meera's School Bell
story.text: Amma points to the clock. 'The short hand is on 8 and the long hand is straight up on 12,' she says. 'What time is it?' Meera remembers: long hand on 12 means o'clock. '8 o'clock — time for school!' Later the long hand has walked halfway round to the 6. 'Now it's half past 8,' Meera grins, 'thirty minutes gone!'
story.extractedProblem: The short hand is on 8 and the long hand points to 12. What time is it?
story.answerInStory: 8 o'clock — the long hand on 12 means a whole hour, no extra minutes.

visual.caption: Long hand UP on 12 = o'clock. Long hand DOWN on 6 = half past. The short hand tells you which hour.

teachingGallery.title: Where is the long hand?
teachingGallery.note: Long hand on 12 = o'clock; on 6 = half past. The short hand names the hour.

examples.caption: Long hand on 12, short hand on 6 → 6 o'clock.
Long hand on 6 → half past; the short hand sits between 9 and 10, so the hour is 9.
Both hands straight up → 12 o'clock (noon or midnight).

standardMethod.summary: Read the SHORT hand for the hour and the LONG hand for the minutes. Long hand on 12 = o'clock; long hand on 6 = half past (30 minutes).
steps: Find the SHORT hand — it points to (or just past) the hour number.
Find the LONG hand — where is it pointing?
Long hand straight UP on 12 → it is that hour 'o'clock'.
Long hand straight DOWN on 6 → 'half past' that hour (30 minutes).
Remember: 60 minutes make one whole hour.

workedExamples.problem: The long hand points to 12 and the short hand to 3. What time is it?
steps: Long hand on 12 means o'clock — no extra minutes.
Short hand on 3 names the hour.

workedExamples.problem: How many minutes are there in one hour?
steps: The long hand walks all the way around once in an hour.

commonMistakes.mistake: Reading the LONG hand for the hour and the SHORT hand for minutes.
commonMistakes.fix: The SHORT hand is the hour; the LONG hand is the minutes. Short = hour (short word), long = minutes.

commonMistakes.mistake: At half past 4, reading the hour as 5 because the short hand has moved.
commonMistakes.fix: At half past, the short hand sits BETWEEN two numbers — always name the SMALLER one. Between 4 and 5 → half past 4.

easy.q: When it is 'o'clock', the long (minute) hand points to which number?
How many minutes are in one hour?
hintLadder: The long hand goes all the way round once.

medium.q: At 'half past', the long hand points to which number?
How many minutes is 'half past' (half an hour)?

challenge.q: The long hand points to 12 and the short hand to 7. What time is it?
hintLadder: The SHORT hand names the hour.

challenge.q: The short hand is between 4 and 5, the long hand on 6. What time is it?

questions.q: For 'o'clock', the long hand is on which number?

teachBackPrompt: Point to a real clock (or draw one) for a grown-up. Show the short hand (hour) and long hand (minutes). Make it '3 o'clock' (long hand up) and 'half past 3' (long hand down), and explain there are 60 minutes in an hour.

revisionCard.summary: Short hand = HOUR, long hand = MINUTES. Long hand on 12 = 'o'clock' (a whole hour). Long hand on 6 = 'half past' (30 minutes). 60 minutes make one hour. At half past, the short hand is between two numbers — name the smaller one.
